KELVIN H, BOOry. JR.
COUNry COUNSEL
December 11, 1991
John Wallace, Staff Attorney
Califomia Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
Re: Request for Informal Assistance
Dear Mr. Wallace:
Following up on our previous telephone conversations, we are requesting informal
assistarce with regard to the participation of Alameda County Supervisor Warren Widener on a
task force appointed by Oakland Mayor Elihu Harris "on fire rebuilding solutions."
Supervisor Widener's home was destroyed in the October 20-21 ftre in the Oakland-
Berkeley hills and he was subsequently requested by Mayor Harris to serve on the City of
Oakland Emergency Preparedness and Community Restoration Task Force. As stated in
Supervisor Widener's letter to us, dated December 6, 1991, he is not representing Alameda County
on the task force but was rather appointed because the mayor believed that, having lost his home
in the fire, he would be especially sensitive to the feelings of Oakland residents who were trying
to deal with ard recover from their losses caused by the fire. The task force was referred to on
page 5 of the November 14, 1991 advice requesr of Oakland City Attomey Jayne Williams to
your division.
We have fumished Supervisor Widener a copy of Chairman Davidian's letter of
November 26, l99l to Ms. Williams but, as you have suggested, we have pointed out that that
letter does not compietely resolve the conflict issues with respect ro Supervisor Widener. Thus,
Chairman Davidian's letter sheds no light on whether Supervisor Widener may parricipate as a
member of the Board of Supervisors in acting on flre-related matters which might come before
it for action. For instance, it is possible that the City might request the County to establish a
county service area to provide for the undergrounding of public utilities in the area devastated by
the fire. We have advised Supervisor Widener that it would not be appropriate for him to
participate in or to influence other Board members with respect to those few fire-related issues
in which he might have a financial interest and which might come before the Board.

John Wallace , Staff Anomey
December ll, 1991
Page 2
The second issue was suggested by you based upon regulation 18700.1(c) if Supervisor
Widener acts or purports to act on behatf ol or as a representative of, the County or its Board
of Supervisors in his service on the mayor's task force. The enclosed letter from Supervisor
Widener emphatically states that he is not so acting. It is our opinion that he is accordingly not
disqualified from serving fully as a member of the mayor's task force so long as he does not
participate or influence other members in connection with issues which might provide a particular
benefit for his home site over those of others. We enclose a copy of our advice letter to
Supervisor Widener.
Very truly yours,
KELVIN H. BOOTY, JR.
County Counsel

BOARD

SUPERVISORS
WARREN WIO ENER
December 6, 1991
Kelvin Booty
County Counsel
Fourth Floor, Administrative Building
1221 Oak SEeet
Oakland, CA 94612
' 
Re: ConJlict of lnteresr-Oakland Fire Task Force
Dear Kelvin:
This letter is in response to your letter of December 2, 1991 requesting a written statement
of my understanding of my role on the City of Oakland Emergency Preparedness and
Community Restoration Task Force.
I do not represent Alameda County. Mayor Harris stated that his major consideration in my
appointment was that I lost my home in the fue and would therefore be especially sensitive
to the feelings of his constituents who were trying to deal with and recover from their loss
from the fire. I would appreciate your proceeding with getting further clarification from the -
Fair Political Practices Commission, as suggested in your letter.
Also, I confirm my position stated to you earlier to advise me prior to any vote that will be
a conflict of interest, or will give the appearance of a conflict of interest.
Very truly yours,
L,Oq1,v,^-
Warren Widener

COUNTY 
COUNSEL
FOURTH FLOOR, ADMINISTBATION BUILDING, 1221 OAK STBEET
OAKLAND. CAL|FORNlAs4612. TELEPHONE(,6d9272-6700. FAX (4651272-s02o
KELVIN H. BOOry, JR.
COUNTY COUNSEL
December 2, 1991
Honorable Warren Widener
Supervisor, Fifth District
1221 Oak Street, Suite 536
Oakland, CA 94612
Re: Conflict of Interest--Oakland Fire Task Force
Dear Supervisor Widener:
The Fair Political Practices Commission has released its response to the request of the Ciry
of Oakland City Attorney for advice with respect to the application of the conflict of inrerest
provisions of the Political Reform Act to various city officials whose homes were destroyed in
the Oaklard-Berkeley Hills fire.
The Commission's response, a copy of which is anached, generaily ukes the position that,
as provided in Govemment Code section 87103, those officials do not have a conflict of interest
because, even if the decisions made by them may have a material financial effect, that effect is
not distinguishable from its effect on the public generally. Regulation section 18703, which-
implements section 87i03, states that no conllict of interest exists if the financial effect of a
govemmental decision on that person's interests "will affect all members of the public or a
significant segment of the public . . .."
The response concludes:
Thus, the number of owners of single family residences that will be affected by the
rebuilding decisions in a substantially similar manner is significantly greater than
the 5 percent of homeowners in the f,re-damaged fiea, and will constitute a
significant segmenr of the public. Accordingly, public officials who have homes
in the affected areas are not disqualified from making or panicipating in making
decisions which will have a material finarcial effect on their real property interest-
-their residence--since *re effect is the same as the effect on a significant segment
of the public.
The FPPC letter goes on ro nore that such a public official may make, panicipate in making, or
attempt to influence a decision only if the decision does not affect ttre public official in a unique
manner, such as a recommendation that a park be built near the official's residence.

Honorable Warren Widener
December 2, 1991

Unfortunately, this letter does not resolve all of the conflict issues with respect to you.
First, it does not at all respond to your role as a member of ttre Board of Supervisors in acting
on matters which come before that body for action. Based upon our discussion with Mr. John
Wallace, one of the senior attomeys on the FPPC staff, it is not likely that ttre Commission would
apply the section 18703 excoption in that situation. While it did conclude that a significant
segment of the public in Oakland was affected by &e fue, it is unlikely that it would similarly
conclude that a significant segment of the Counry as a whole was so affected. This means that
it would not be appropriate for you to either participate in or to influence other Board members
with respect to those few fre-related issues which may come before &e Board of Supewisors.
Second, Mr. Wallace has suggested that under Regulation 18700.1(c), g copy of which is
attached, you may have a conflict of"ilt.r"st eu"n irrlartic"ipating on the mayor's task force if you
act or purport to act on behalf ol or as the rcpresentative of, the County or its Board of
Supervisors. In our discussion with the staff attorney, we stated our understanding that you are
not serving on the task force as a member of the Board of Supervisors or as any other kind of
representative of the County, and tlat you were appointed because you are a person whose
experience and offices r,Mith both the City of Berkeley and ttre County of Alameda have provided
you with experience which would be valuable to the mayor in carrying out the duties of the task
force.
Mr. Wallace has urged us to submit a written request for informal assistance in your behalf
because of the fact ttrat a rosponse to the City Attomey does not cover these additional issuesl
He assures us that the matter will be given priority and rhe reply expedited. If you would like
us to proceed further in your behalf, we would appreciate your stating in writing your
understanding that your role on ttre task force is not ttrat of an elected County official or any
other representative of ttre County if that is correct.
Very truly yours,
KELVIN H. BOOTY, JR.
County Counsel

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Alameda County Supervisor Warren Widener, whose home was destroyed in the October 1991 Oakland-Berkeley Hills fire, may participate as a member of the Oakland Emergency Preparedness and Community Restoration Task Force without conflict of interest, and whether his participation in the task force as a private citizen (not representing the County) creates any disqualification under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Alameda County Counsel to the FPPC requesting informal assistance, not an FPPC response letter. The document includes supporting letters from Supervisor Widener and the County Counsel's initial advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present in the request letter itself, though the substance is clearly outlined in the correspondence.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether Alameda County Supervisor Warren Widener, whose home was destroyed in the October 1991 Oakland-Berkeley Hills fire, may participate as a member of the Oakland Emergency Preparedness and Community Restoration Task Force without conflict of interest, and whether his participation in the task force as a private citizen (not representing the County) creates any disqualification under the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: Alameda County Counsel requests informal assistance from the FPPC regarding whether Supervisor Warren Widener may serve on an Oakland fire rebuilding task force after losing his home in the fire, specifically addressing potential conflicts of interest both on the task force and in his county supervisor role on fire-related matters that might come before the Board.
